title: Wholesome Algerian Lentil Pudding
description: A sweet, malty lentil pudding with a glassy, jelly-like texture, made with ground clove and maple syrup, perfect for kids and Jain diners.
instructions: Rinse 200g red lentils under cold water until the water runs clear.
In a medium saucepan combine the rinsed lentils and 400ml water, then bring to a gentle boil.
Reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer for 20 minutes until the lentils are tender but not mushy.
Drain any excess liquid, then return the lentils to the saucepan and add 50g sugar, 1g ground clove, 1 tsp orange zest, and 1 tsp vanilla.
Stir the mixture until the sugar dissolves and the flavor is aromatic.
Pour in 20g maple syrup and 1 tsp sea salt, then cook over low heat for another 15 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Remove from heat and allow the pudding to cool to room temperature, letting it thicken into a glassy consistency.
Spoon the pudding into serving glasses, chill for at least 2 hours, and serve with a sprinkle of ground clove.
